TSLA: Direxion Daily TSLA Bear 1X Shares (NASDAQ:TSLS) Short Interest Down 65.1% in January. Direxion Daily TSLA Bear 1X Shares (TSLS) experienced a significant 65.1% decrease in short interest during January, falling to 1,641,008 shares by January 30th from 4,696,117 shares on January 15th. The stock recently announced a 1-10 reverse split effective March 5th and saw a quarterly dividend increase. Institutional investors have adjusted their positions in the company, with several increasing their stakes.

Here are Wednesday's biggest analyst calls: Nvidia, Tesla, Dick's, Netflix, Kingsoft, Robinhood & more. This article details the biggest analyst calls on Wall Street for Wednesday, February 11, 2026. It includes upgrades, initiations, and reiterations for companies like Nvidia, Tesla, Kingsoft Cloud, BridgeBio Oncology, Energy Fuels, James Hardie Industries, Samsara, Oracle, Norwegian, Mattel, Dick's Sporting Goods, Beta Technologies, Cloudflare, ArcelorMittal, McGrath RentCorp, Porch Group, Micron, Robinhood, Netflix, and Tesla. Analysts from firms such as Goldman Sachs, Stifel, JPMorgan, UBS, Bernstein, Barclays, Baird, and Jefferies provide their insights and price targets.

Tesla To Charge $300,000 For Long-Range Semi, Undercutting Rivals By Over $145,000: Report. Tesla is reportedly pricing its Long-Range Semi at $300,000 and the Standard trim at $260,000 for 2026 deliveries. This pricing strategy significantly undercuts rivals in the zero-emission truck market, where the average price hovers around $435,000. CEO Elon Musk has indicated high-volume production for the Semi will begin this year, and Tesla has already delivered units to DHL and partners with Uber Freight and PepsiCo.

Tencent, Tesla team up on WeChat-linked in-car features in China. Tencent Cloud has announced a partnership with U.S. automaker Tesla to integrate WeChat-linked in-car features into Tesla vehicles in China. These new features will include instant transfer of WeChat location data and smart services suggestions based on destination. The upgrades will be rolled out via over-the-air updates to existing Model 3 and Y vehicles and will be standard in all future Tesla vehicles sold in China.

A Waco company is taking Tesla and X to federal court over voice tech. A Waco-based company, Songbird Tech LLC, is suing Elon Musk's Tesla and X for patent infringement related to voice assistance technology. The lawsuit, filed in Austin, alleges that Tesla's vehicles, including the Cybertruck, and X's voice messaging features infringe on its 2014 patent. Songbird Tech has previously filed similar lawsuits against other companies, leading to accusations of being a "patent troll."

Tesla Rival BYD Unveils New Battery Tech Promising 10,000-Cycle Lifespan, Eyes Solid-State Production By 2027. Chinese EV manufacturer BYD has announced significant advancements in battery technology, developing sodium-ion batteries capable of 10,000 charging cycles, far exceeding current industry standards. The company also aims for small-scale production of sulfide solid-state batteries by 2027, which would offer enhanced lifespan and faster charging. This news comes as BYD faces a recent stock sell-off due to lower January sales, while Tesla also reports its own battery chemistry breakthrough with dry electrode processes.

US Retail Sales Disappoint; Costco and Walmart Shares Fall. U.S. retail sales data for December fell short of expectations, negatively impacting major stock indices. While the Dow Jones Industrial Average saw a slight gain, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq declined, with retail giants Costco and Walmart experiencing share drops. Conversely, Disney, Home Depot, and Tesla saw gains, and Spotify's stock soared after a significant increase in its fourth-quarter net profit.

Strategic Shift: ARK’s Space and Defense ETF Adds Tesla Amid Investor Outflows. Cathie Wood's ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF has strategically added Tesla shares to its portfolio, signaling a shift towards robotics and autonomous systems, despite experiencing significant investor outflows. Renamed the ARK Space & Defense Innovation ETF, the fund's investment in Tesla is viewed as a bet on the "Optimus" humanoid robotics project and highlights the convergence of space, defense, and advanced robotics. The fund is facing headwinds with a substantial net outflow and is repositioning its core holdings while maintaining a defensive and aerospace-oriented focus.

Analysts Are Bullish on These Consumer Cyclical Stocks: Peloton Interactive (PTON), Ferrari (RACE). This article highlights recent bullish sentiments from analysts for several Consumer Cyclical stocks, including Peloton Interactive (PTON), Ferrari (RACE), and Tesla (TSLA). Roth MKM reiterated a Buy rating for Peloton with a $10.00 price target, while Bernstein maintained a Buy rating for Ferrari with a $395.00 price target. Mizuho Securities reiterated a Buy rating for Tesla with a price target of $540.00, despite the analyst consensus being a Hold.

Tesla Semi to Offer 2 Range Options, Company Says. Tesla has confirmed it will offer two range options for its Semi electric Class 8 tractor: a standard 325-mile version and a long-range 500-mile version. Both variants will feature three independent motors, achieve 1.7 kilowatt-hours per mile in energy consumption, and charge up to 60% in 30 minutes. High-volume production is expected to begin this year at the Reno factory, with infrastructure for charging and sales also being expanded through partnerships with companies like Pilot Travel Centers and Uber Freight.

Morgan Stanley says solar manufacturing could add as much as $50 billion in value to Tesla. Morgan Stanley suggests that Tesla's move into solar manufacturing could increase its energy business valuation by $25 billion to $50 billion. The bank estimates the solar panel endeavor will cost Tesla $30 billion to $70 billion, but expects the company to vertically integrate solar capacity to meet internal demand, such as for data centers, rather than compete directly in the broader market. This strategy aims to avoid supply chain issues and leverage a combined solar and energy storage business.

Tesla files complaint against union member for secretly recording works council meeting, memo shows. Tesla has filed a criminal complaint against a member of Germany's IG Metall trade union for secretly recording a works council meeting at its Gruenheide plant near Berlin. The company confirmed the incident, stating that the external IG Metall member, attending as a guest, recorded the non-public event with his computer, which Tesla considers a criminal offense. This complaint further underscores the strained relationship between Tesla and the IG Metall union.
TGT: ING Groep NV Sells 46,148 Shares of Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST. ING Groep NV reduced its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation by 33.0% in the third quarter, selling 46,148 shares and leaving them with 93,564 shares valued at $86.6 million. Despite this, other institutions like Vanguard Group and State Street Corp increased their holdings. Analyst ratings for Costco mostly lean towards "Buy" or "Hold," with a consensus target price of $1,009.00.

Target cuts 500 jobs, invests more money in store staffing. Target announced it is eliminating approximately 500 office and supply chain jobs while simultaneously investing more money in store staffing, training, and guest experience. This strategic shift aims to improve customer service and boost sales, which have been sluggish in recent years. The company's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, is focusing on accelerating growth through improved merchandise, in-store experience, and technology.

Target shakes up its executive ranks as new CEO takes charge. Target has announced significant executive leadership changes following Michael Fiddelke's appointment as CEO. Cara Sylvester will become the chief merchandising officer, and Lisa Roath has been named chief operating officer. These changes come as part of a restructuring and will see Rick Gomez and Jill Sando depart the company.
SBUX: Alps Advisors Inc. Decreases Holdings in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Alps Advisors Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 59.6% in the third quarter, holding 23,304 shares worth approximately $1.97 million after selling 34,362 shares. Despite missing Q3 EPS estimates, Starbucks exceeded revenue expectations and provided FY2026 guidance. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts with a target price of $104.67, while institutional investors own 72.29% of its stock.
DIS: Sky and Disney announce expanded multi-year agreement, making Disney+ available to millions more customers in the UK & Ireland. Sky and Disney have announced an expanded multi-year agreement that will integrate Disney+ into millions of Sky TV subscriptions in the UK and Ireland starting March 2026. The deal includes a new Disney+ Cinema channel for Sky Cinema customers and aims to make Disney+'s content more accessible to a wider audience through Sky's platform. This collaboration builds on a long-standing relationship and seeks to enhance the entertainment offerings for Sky subscribers.

Impax Asset Management Group plc Sells 14,123 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Impax Asset Management Group plc reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 6.2% in the third quarter, selling 14,123 shares and leaving them with 215,505 shares valued at $24.675 million. Despite this, institutional investors and hedge funds still own a significant 65.71% of Disney's stock. The article also highlights recent positive and negative sentiment regarding Disney, including leadership changes, analyst ratings, park investments, content updates, a reported $170 million loss on "Snow White," and scrutiny over executive compensation.

US Retail Sales Disappoint; Costco and Walmart Shares Fall. U.S. retail sales data for December fell short of expectations, negatively impacting major stock indices. While the Dow Jones Industrial Average saw a slight gain, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq declined, with retail giants Costco and Walmart experiencing share drops. Conversely, Disney, Home Depot, and Tesla saw gains, and Spotify's stock soared after a significant increase in its fourth-quarter net profit.

Disney Kicks Off Its First Investment-Grade Bond Sale Since 2020. Walt Disney Co. is issuing investment-grade dollar bonds for the first time since 2020, joining other companies in taking advantage of favorable borrowing terms. The offering is expected to be in as many as four parts, with maturities ranging from three to ten years, and proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes. The initial price talk for the longest maturity note indicates a premium of 0.85 percentage point above comparable Treasuries.
NKE: Under Armour Faces Data Breach Affecting 72 Million Customers. Under Armour is confronting a significant data breach, reportedly affecting 72 million customers and linked to the Everest ransomware gang. This incident has led to a 7.83% drop in Under Armour (NYSE: UA) Class C stock and has raised concerns about the company's financial stability, data protection, and market reputation. The company is actively investigating the breach and its financial and legal ramifications amidst already turbulent financials and competitive pressures from rivals like Nike and Adidas.
